customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high specificity. Customer is not skeptical of solar — he owns a system. His concern is informational: he wants to understand cost, timeline, and process for a battery add-on before committing to anything further. He is not ready for a proposal frame.
unresolved_concerns: Customer explicitly stated he is not ready for a site visit — appointment must be framed as an education call on cost and timeline, not a proposal or design review.

Name mismatch: customer identified as 'Parker' at call open; rep closed by calling him 'Alex.' Lead record may be mismatched. Closer must confirm the customer's actual name before the appointment and address any CRM discrepancy.
Orphaned flow incomplete: panel size and system working status were not captured. Closer should confirm the existing Freedom Solar system is operational before scoping the battery add-on.
Credit qualifier applicability to battery-only installs is unconfirmed in the schema — closer should be aware this may need re-verification depending on Ion's financing rules for add-ons.
sensitive_topics_raised: Existing solar system installed by Freedom Solar (different company) — customer owns it outright, not leased.
Customer's explicit 'not ready to commit' framing — closer must earn the right to present before moving toward any proposal.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Tuesday at 4PM as a phone call (not a site visit).
Rep will send a text with the date, time, and rep's contact number after the call.
closer_should_know: This customer expects to be educated on battery backup cost and timeline before any proposal framing. He said it twice. The closer should open by validating that expectation — confirm the call is about walking him through options and answering his questions — and earn the right to present before moving toward design or pricing. Also: confirm the customer's name is Parker, not Alex, before the call begins.
